diagrams.title: C++ Standard Exception Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Shows the complete hierarchy of standard exception classes in C++
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the relationship between exception classes and which catch blocks to use for different error types.

diagrams.title: Exception Flow and Stack Unwinding
diagrams.description: Demonstrates how exceptions propagate through function call stack
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing the path of exception propagation and understanding automatic cleanup through destructors.

diagrams.title: Catch Block Matching in Class Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Shows how exception type matching works with derived classes
diagrams.purpose: Understanding why catch block order matters and preventing unreachable catch blocks.

diagrams.title: Object Slicing Problem
diagrams.description: Illustrates what happens when catching by value vs by reference
elements: Derived object with base and derived parts
Caught by value: only base part copied (sliced)
Caught by reference: full object preserved
Caught by pointer: full object through pointer
diagrams.purpose: Understanding why catching by reference prevents loss of derived class information.
